zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Neale於1549年2月1日出生在英國倫敦時，她的父親Thomas Neale II44歲，母親Goditha Throckmorton45歲。她於1565年與Henry St John在英國倫敦結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和13個女兒的父母。她去世於1618年9月7日在英國貝德福爾凱享年69歲，埋葬在英國貝德福爾凱。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane Neale于1549年2月1日出生在英国倫敦时，她的父亲Thomas Neale II44岁，母亲Goditha Throckmorton45岁。她于1565年与Henry St John在英国倫敦结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和13个女儿的父母。她去世于1618年9月7日在英国貝德福爾凱享年69岁，埋葬在英国貝德福爾凱。

nameMeanings.name: Neale
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Neal
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Old French, Anglo-Norman French, and Middle English personal name Neel, Nele, Nihel, Niel, itself derived from the Latin name Nigellus (a diminutive of Latin niger ‘black’), originally a nickname for someone with black hair or a dark complexion. The name was very common among Normans and was brought to England at the time of the Norman Conquest. There has been considerable confusion with the Irish and Scottish Gaelic name Niall (see Neil ); the two names are now pronounced identically. It is theoretically possible that in Normandy, where the personal name was popular, that it was also used for Old Norse Njáll, but this is difficult to prove. Njáll was adopted from the Irish Gaelic personal name Niall by Vikings in Ireland, who took it back to Iceland and Norway, but whether the Vikings also took Njáll to Normandy and to the northwest of England, is an open question, which cannot be settled on the available evidence.
English: alternatively from the Middle English personal name Nele, a variant of Nell as a pet form of Elias (see Ellis ). Compare Nelson , Nielson .
Scottish and Irish: shortened form of   McNeal   (see   McNeil  ).

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Neale was born on 1 February 1549, in London, England, her father, Thomas Neale II, was 44 and her mother, Goditha Throckmorton, was 45. She married Henry St John in 1565, in London, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 13 daughters. She died on 7 September 1618, in Keysoe, Bedfordshire, England, at the age of 69, and was buried in Keysoe, Bedfordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Neale est née le 1 février 1549, à Londres, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Neale II, avait 44 ans et sa mère, Goditha Throckmorton, avait 45 ans. Elle a épousé Henry St John en 1565, à Londres, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 13 filles. Elle est décédée le 7 septembre 1618, à Keysoe, Bedfordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 69 ans, et a été enterrée à Keysoe, Bedfordshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jane Neale am 1. Februar 1549 in London, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Thomas Neale II 44 und ihre Mutter Goditha Throckmorton 45. Sie hat 1565 in London, England Henry St John geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 13 Töchter. Sie starb am 7. September 1618 im Alter von 69 Jahren in Keysoe, Bedfordshire, England und wurde in Keysoe, Bedfordshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Neale è nata il il 1 febbraio 1549 nel London, England, sua padre, Thomas Neale II, aveva 44 anni e sua madre, Goditha Throckmorton, aveva 45 anni. Lei sposato Henry St John nel 1565, nel London, England. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 13 figlie. Morì il 7 settembre 1618, nel Keysoe, Bedfordshire, England, all'età di 69 anni, e fu sepolta a Keysoe, Bedfordshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Nealeが1549年2月1日にイギリスロンドンで生まれた時，父Thomas Neale IIは44歳，母Goditha Throckmortonは45歳であった。1565年にイギリスロンドンでHenry St Johnと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と13人の娘の親であった。彼女は1618年9月7日にイギリスベドフォードシュアイアケイソで69歳で死去し，イギリスベドフォードシュアイアケイソにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Neale(이)가 1549년 2월 1일 영국 런던에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Neale II(은)는 44세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Goditha Throckmorton(은)는 45세였다. 그녀는 1565년 영국 런던에서 Henry St John(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 열 명 이상의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1618년 9월 7일 영국 베드퍼드실르 케이소에서 69세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 베드퍼드실르 케이소에 묻혔다.

pt.1 February 1549–7 September 1618: 1 de fevereiro de 1549–7 de setembro de 1618
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Neale nasceu em 1 de fevereiro de 1549, em London, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Neale II, tinha 44 anos e sua mãe, Goditha Throckmorton, tinha 45 anos. Ela casou-se com Henry St John em 1565, em London, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 13 filhas. Ela faleceu em 7 de setembro de 1618, em Keysoe, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, com 69 anos, e foi sepultada em Keysoe, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Neale родилась 1 февраля 1549 г. в местности Лондон, Англия, её отцу по имени Thomas Neale II было 44 года, а матери, которую звали Goditha Throckmorton, 45. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Henry St John в 1565 г. в местности Лондон, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 13 дочерей. Она умерла 7 сентября 1618 г. в местности кисо, бедфордшир, Англия в возрасте 69 лет и была похоронена в местности кисо, бедфордшир, Англия.

es.1 February 1549–7 September 1618: 1 de febrero de 1549–7 de septiembre de 1618
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jane Neale nació el 1 de febrero de 1549, en London, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas Neale II, tenía 44 años y su mamá, Goditha Throckmorton, tenía 45 años. Se casó con Henry St John en 1565, en London, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 13 hijas. Murió el 7 de septiembre de 1618, en Keysoe, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 69 años, y fue sepultada en Keysoe, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra.
